message.content:
Search the codebase thoroughly for any existing definitions or lemmas related to:
1. Sidon sets (B2 sets)
2. Asymptotic bases (of any order)
3. Sum-free sets or additive combinatorics concepts

Look in both FormalConjectures/ and in .lake/packages/mathlib/ (for Mathlib definitions).

Search for keywords: "Sidon", "sidon", "B2", "asymptotic basis", "AsymptoticBasis", "additive basis", "sumset", "pairwise sums", "IsInfiniteSidonSet", "IsAsymptoticBasisOrder3".

Report all findings with file paths and relevant code snippets. Be very thorough - check naming conventions like camelCase, snake_case, etc.

    26â†’/--
    27â†’A set $A$ is said to be sum-free if the sumset $A + A$ is disjoint from $A$, i.e.
    28â†’if the equation $a + b = c$ has no solution with $a, b, c \in A$.

    32â†’/-- A set $A$ of natural numbers is said to have bounded gaps if there exists an integer $p$ such
    33â†’that $A âˆ© [n, n + 1, ..., n + p]$ is nonempty for all $n$. -/

    36â†’/-- A Sidon set is a set, such that such that all pairwise sums of elements are distinct apart from
    37â†’coincidences forced by the commutativity of addition. -/

    39â†’  iâ‚پ + iâ‚‚ = jâ‚پ + jâ‚‚ â†’ (iâ‚پ = jâ‚پ âˆ§ iâ‚‚ = jâ‚‚) âˆ¨ (iâ‚پ = jâ‚‚ âˆ§ iâ‚‚ = jâ‚پ)

    44â†’    {Y : Set â„•} (hY : Y.IsAPOfLength 3) :

    54â†’  have haâ‚پ : a + d âˆˆ A := mem_of_mem_inter_left <| hss (hY â–¸ âں¨1, by norm_num, by simpâں©)

    61â†’theorem IsSidon.subset {A B : Set خ±} (hB : IsSidon B) (hAB : A âٹ† B) : IsSidon A :=
    62â†’  fun _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ â†¦ hB _ (hAB â€¹_â€؛) _ (hAB â€¹_â€؛) _ (hAB â€¹_â€؛) _ (hAB â€¹_â€؛) â€¹_â€؛

    64â†’theorem IsSidon.insert {A : Set خ±} {m : خ±} [IsRightCancelAdd خ±] [IsLeftCancelAdd خ±]
    65â†’    (hA : IsSidon A) :
    66â†’    IsSidon (A âˆھ {m}) â†” (m âˆˆ A âˆ¨ âˆ€لµ‰ (a âˆˆ A) (b âˆˆ A), m + m â‰  a + b âˆ§ âˆ€ c âˆˆ A, m + a â‰  b + c) := by
    67â†’  by_cases h_mem : m âˆˆ A
    68â†’  آ· exact âں¨fun _ â†¦ .inl h_mem, fun _ â†¦ by rwa [union_singleton, insert_eq_of_mem h_mem]âں©
    69â†’  refine âں¨fun h â†¦ .inr fun a ha b hb â†¦ âں¨fun hc â†¦ ?_, fun c hc h_contr â†¦ ?_âں©, fun hm â†¦ ?_âں©
    70â†’  آ· exact h m (by simp) a (by simp [ha]) m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) hc
    71â†’      |>.elim (fun _ â†¦ by simp_all) (fun _ â†¦ by simp_all)
    72â†’  آ· exact h m (by simp) b (by simp [hb]) a (by simp [ha]) c (by simp [hc]) h_contr
    73â†’      |>.elim (fun _ â†¦ by simp_all) (fun _ â†¦ by simp_all)
    74â†’  آ· intro iâ‚پ hiâ‚پ
    75â†’    rcases hiâ‚پ with (hiâ‚پ | hiâ‚پ)
    76â†’    آ· intro jâ‚پ hjâ‚پ
    77â†’      rcases hjâ‚پ with (hjâ‚پ | hjâ‚پ)
    78â†’      آ· intro iâ‚‚ hiâ‚‚
    79â†’        rcases hiâ‚‚ with (hiâ‚‚ | hiâ‚‚)
    80â†’        آ· intro jâ‚‚ hjâ‚‚

    83â†’          آ· simp_all
    84â†’            exact fun h â†¦ by cases (hm jâ‚پ hjâ‚پ iâ‚پ hiâ‚پ).2 iâ‚‚ hiâ‚‚ (add_comm jâ‚پ m â–¸ h.symm)
    85â†’        آ· simp_all

    87â†’      آ· simp_all

    89â†’        آ· cases (hm iâ‚پ hiâ‚پ b hb).1 h.symm

    95â†’/-!
    96â†’Maximal Sidon sets in an interval.
    97â†’
    98â†’We follow the convention that `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A âٹ† {1, â€¦, N}` is Sidon and
    99â†’is inclusion-maximal among subsets of `Set.Icc 1 N` with the Sidon property.

   101â†’
   102â†’/-- `IsMaximalSidonSetIn A N` means `A âٹ† {1, â€¦, N}` is Sidon and cannot be extended within
   103â†’`{1, â€¦, N}` while remaining Sidon. -/

   105â†’  A âٹ† Set.Icc 1 N âˆ§ IsSidon A âˆ§
   106â†’    âˆ€ â¦ƒx : â„•â¦„, x âˆˆ Set.Icc 1 N â†’ x âˆ‰ A â†’ آ¬ IsSidon (A âˆھ {x})
   107â†’

   110â†’/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, â€¦, N}`, then `A âٹ† {1, â€¦, N}`. -/

   112â†’    A âٹ† Set.Icc 1 N := hA.1

   114â†’/-- If `A` is a maximal Sidon set in `{1, â€¦, N}`, then `A` is Sidon. -/

   116â†’
   117â†’/-- Maximality condition unpacked. -/

   119â†’    (hx : x âˆˆ Set.Icc 1 N) (hxA : x âˆ‰ A) : آ¬ IsSidon (A âˆھ {x}) := hA.2.2 hx hxA

   127â†’instance (A : Finset خ±) [DecidableEq خ±] : Decidable (IsSidon (A : Set خ±)) :=
   128â†’  decidable_of_iff (âˆ€لµ‰ (iâ‚پ âˆˆ A) (jâ‚پ âˆˆ A) (iâ‚‚ âˆˆ A) (jâ‚‚ âˆˆ A), _) <| by rfl

   130â†’/-- The maximum size of a Sidon set in the supplied `Finset`. -/

   133â†’
   134â†’/-- If `A` is finite Sidon, then `A âˆھ {s}` is also Sidon provided `s â‰¥ A.max + 1`. -/
   135â†’theorem IsSidon.insert_ge_max' {A : Finset â„•} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set â„•)) {s : â„•}
   136â†’    (hs : 2 * A.max' h + 1 â‰¤ s) :
   137â†’    IsSidon (A âˆھ {s}) := by
   138â†’  have hâ‚پ {a b c : â„•} (ha : a âˆˆ A) (hb : b âˆˆ A) (hc : c âˆˆ A) :
   139â†’        a + b < 2 * A.max' h + 1 + c := by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb]
   140â†’  have : s âˆ‰ A := by
   141â†’    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ â€¹_â€؛ |>.2 fun a ha â†¦ by
   142â†’      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   143â†’  exact (IsSidon.insert hA).2 <| by simpa [this] using fun a ha b hb â†¦
   144â†’    âں¨by linarith [A.le_max' _ ha, A.le_max' _ hb], fun c hc â†¦ by linarith [hâ‚پ hc hb ha]âں©
   145â†’
   146â†’theorem IsSidon.exists_insert {A : Finset â„•} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set â„•)) :
   147â†’    âˆƒ m âˆ‰ A, IsSidon (A âˆھ {m}) := by
   148â†’  refine âں¨2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, insert_ge_max' h hA le_rflâں©
   149â†’  exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ â€¹_â€؛ |>.2 fun a ha â†¦ by
   150â†’    linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   151â†’
   152â†’theorem IsSidon.exists_insert_ge {A : Finset â„•} (h : A.Nonempty) (hA : IsSidon (A : Set â„•)) (s : â„•) :
   153â†’    âˆƒ m â‰¥ s, m âˆ‰ A âˆ§ IsSidon (A âˆھ {m}) := by
   154â†’  refine âں¨if s â‰¥ 2 * A.max' h + 1 then s else 2 * A.max' h + 1, ?_, ?_, ?_âں©
   155â†’  آ· split_ifs <;> linarith
   156â†’  آ· split_ifs <;>
   157â†’    exact mt (A.le_max' _) <| not_le.2 <| Finset.max'_lt_iff _ â€¹_â€؛ |>.2 fun a ha â†¦ by
   158â†’      linarith [A.le_max' _ ha]
   159â†’  آ· split_ifs with hs
   160â†’    آ· exact insert_ge_max' h hA hs
   161â†’    آ· exact insert_ge_max' h hA le_rfl

    19â†’/-!
    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 157
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/157](https://www.erdosproblems.com/157)
    23â†’
    24â†’Erdإ‘s, Sأ،rkأ¶zy, and Sأ³s asked whether there exists an infinite Sidon set that is also an
    25â†’asymptotic basis of order 3. This was answered affirmatively by Pilatte.
    26â†’
    27â†’[ESS94] Erdإ‘s, P., Sأ،rkأ¶zy, A. and Sأ³s, V. T., *On sum sets of Sidon sets*. J. Number Theory
    28â†’(1994).
    29â†’
    30â†’[Er94b] Erdإ‘s, P., *Some of my favourite problems in various branches of combinatorics*.
    31â†’Matematiche (Catania) (1994).
    32â†’
    33â†’[Pi23] Pilatte, C., *Sidon sets are asymptotic bases of order 3*. (2023).
    34â†’-/
    35â†’
    36â†’open Filter Set
    37â†’
    38â†’namespace Erdos157
    39â†’
    40â†’/-- An infinite set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a Sidon set ($B_2$ set) if all pairwise sums
    41â†’are distinct: whenever $a + b = c + d$ with $a, b, c, d \in A$, we have
    42â†’$\{a, b\} = \{c, d\}$ as multisets (i.e. either $a = c$ and $b = d$, or
    43â†’$a = d$ and $b = c$). -/
    44â†’def IsInfiniteSidonSet (A : Set â„•) : Prop :=
    45â†’  Set.Infinite A âˆ§
    46â†’  âˆ€ a âˆˆ A, âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, âˆ€ c âˆˆ A, âˆ€ d âˆˆ A,
    47â†’    a + b = c + d â†’ (a = c âˆ§ b = d) âˆ¨ (a = d âˆ§ b = c)
    48â†’
    49â†’/-- A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is an asymptotic basis of order $3$ if every sufficiently
    50â†’large natural number can be represented as a sum of exactly $3$ elements
    51â†’(with repetition allowed) from $A$. -/
    52â†’def IsAsymptoticBasisOrder3 (A : Set â„•) : Prop :=
    53â†’  âˆ€ل¶  n : â„• in atTop, âˆƒ a âˆˆ A, âˆƒ b âˆˆ A, âˆƒ c âˆˆ A, n = a + b + c
    54â†’
    55â†’/--
    56â†’Erdإ‘s Problem 157 [ESS94, Er94b]:
    57â†’
    58â†’Does there exist an infinite Sidon set which is an asymptotic basis of order $3$?
    59â†’
    60â†’A set $A \subseteq \mathbb{N}$ is a Sidon set if all pairwise sums $a + b$ ($a, b \in A$)
    61â†’are distinct. A set $A$ is an asymptotic basis of order $3$ if every sufficiently large
    62â†’integer is the sum of $3$ elements from $A$.
    63â†’
    64â†’Answered YES by Pilatte [Pi23].
    65â†’-/
    66â†’@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    67â†’theorem erdos_157 :
    68â†’    answer(True) â†” âˆƒ A : Set â„•, IsInfiniteSidonSet A âˆ§ IsAsymptoticBasisOrder3 A := by
    69â†’  sorry
    70â†’
    71â†’end Erdos157

    24â†’/-! # Arithmetic Progressions
    25â†’
    26â†’Main definitions:
    27â†’- `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set خ±) (l : â„•âˆ‍) (a d : خ±)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the
    28â†’  set consisting of an arithmetic progression of length `l` (possibly infinite) with first term
    29â†’  `a` and difference `d`. Useful for cases in which additional conditions need to be applied to
    30â†’  the individual terms and/or difference.
    31â†’- `Set.IsAPOfLength (s : Set خ±) (l : â„•âˆ‍)` : predicate asserting that `s` is the set consisting
    32â†’  of an arithmetic progression of length `l`, for some some first term and difference.
    33â†’-/
    34â†’
    35â†’variable {خ± : Type*} [AddCommMonoid خ±]
    36â†’
    37â†’/--
    38â†’A set $S$ is an arithmetic progression of length $l$ with first term $a$ and difference $d$
    39â†’if $S = \{a, a + d, ..., a + (l - 1)d\}$, if $l$ if finite, else $S = \{a, a + d, a + 2d, ...\}.
    40â†’This can be written as `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith a l a d`, where `l : â„•âˆ‍` may take the infinite
    41â†’value `âٹ¤`.
    42â†’-/
    43â†’def Set.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : Set خ±) (l : â„•âˆ‍) (a d : خ±) : Prop :=
    44â†’  ENat.card s = l âˆ§ s = {a + n â€¢ d | (n : â„•) (_ : n < l)}
    45â†’
    46â†’/--
    47â†’A list version of `Set.IsAPOfLengthWith`. Useful when order preservation is required, for example
    48â†’when considering images under arbitrary functions.
    49â†’-/
    50â†’def List.IsAPOfLengthWith (s : List خ±) (l : â„•) (a d : خ±) : Prop :=
    51â†’  s = (List.range l).map (fun n â†¦ a + n â€¢ d) âˆ¨ s = (List.range l).reverse.map (fun n â†¦ a + n â€¢ d)
    52â†’
    53â†’namespace Set.IsAPOfLengthWith
    54â†’
    55â†’variable {s : Set خ±} {l : â„•âˆ‍} {a d : خ±}
    56â†’
    57â†’theorem card (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : ENat.card s = l := h.1
    58â†’theorem eq (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : s = {a + n â€¢ d | (n : â„•) (_ : n < l)} := h.2
    59â†’
    60â†’/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    61â†’if `s` is empty. -/
    62â†’@[simp]
    63â†’theorem zero : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 0 a d â†” s = âˆ… := by simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    64â†’
    65â†’/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length one if and only
    66â†’if `s` is a singleton. -/
    67â†’@[simp]
    68â†’theorem one : s.IsAPOfLengthWith 1 a d â†” s = {a} := by simp +contextual [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    69â†’

    72â†’namespace List.IsAPOfLengthWith

    74â†’variable {s : List خ±} {l : â„•} {a d : خ±}
    75â†’
    76â†’theorem length (h : s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d) : s.length = l := by

    78â†’
    79â†’/-- An arithmetic progression with first term `a` and difference `d` is of length zero if and only
    80â†’if `s` is empty. -/
    81â†’@[simp]

    86â†’if `s` is a singleton. -/

    93â†’/-- In an abelian additive group `خ±`, the set `{a, b}` with `a â‰  b` is an arithmetic progression of
    94â†’length `2` with first term `a` and difference `b - a`. -/

    96â†’    {a b : خ±} (hab : a â‰  b) :

    98â†’  simp [IsAPOfLengthWith]
    99â†’  rw [Finset.card_insert_of_notMem (by simpa only [Finset.mem_singleton])]
   100â†’  simp
   101â†’  refine Set.ext fun x => âں¨fun h â†¦ ?_, fun âں¨n, âں¨_, _âں©âں© â†¦ by interval_cases n <;> simp_allâں©
   102â†’  cases h with
   103â†’  | inl hl => use 0; simp [hl]

   107â†’-- the `AddCommGroup` result above.
   108â†’/-- The set `{a, b} : Set â„•` with `a < b` is an arithmetic progression of length `2` with
   109â†’first term `a` and difference `b - a`. -/

   111â†’    Set.IsAPOfLengthWith {a, b} 2 a (b - a) := by
   112â†’  let âں¨n, hâں© := Nat.exists_eq_add_of_lt hab
   113â†’  simp [Set.IsAPOfLengthWith, h, add_assoc]
   114â†’  exact Set.ext fun x => âں¨fun a => by aesop, fun âں¨w, âں¨_, _âں©âں© => by interval_cases w <;> simp_allâں©
   115â†’
   116â†’/--
   117â†’The predicate that a set `s` is an arithmetic progression of length `l` (possibly infinite).
   118â†’This predicate does not assert a specific value for the first term or the difference of the
   119â†’arithmetic progression.

   121â†’def Set.IsAPOfLength (s : Set خ±) (l : â„•âˆ‍) : Prop :=
   122â†’  âˆƒ a d : خ±, s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d
   123â†’
   124â†’/--
   125â†’A list version of `Set.IsAPOfLength`. Useful when order preservation is required, for example
   126â†’when considering images under arbitrary functions.

   128â†’def List.IsAPOfLength (s : List خ±) (l : â„•) : Prop :=
   129â†’  âˆƒ a d : خ±, s.IsAPOfLengthWith l a d
   130â†’
   131â†’namespace Set.IsAPOfLength
   132â†’

   138â†’

   140â†’  âں¨h.choose, h.choose_spec.choose, h.choose_spec.choose_spec.2âں©

   142â†’/-- Only the empty set is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/

   144â†’
   145â†’/-- Only singletons are finite arithmetic progressions of length $1$. -/
   146â†’@[simp] theorem one : s.IsAPOfLength 1 â†” âˆƒ a, s = {a} := by simp [IsAPOfLength]
   147â†’
   148â†’/-- If a set is an arithmetic progression of lengths `lâ‚پ` and `lâ‚‚`, then the lengths are
   149â†’equal. -/
   150â†’theorem congr {s : Set خ±} {lâ‚پ lâ‚‚ : â„•âˆ‍}

   166â†’
   167â†’/-- Only the empty list is a finite arithmetic progression of length $0$. -/

   169â†’
   170â†’/-- Only singletons are finite arithmetic progressions of length $1$. -/

   172â†’

   182â†’theorem Set.isAPOfLength_pair {خ± : Type*} [DecidableEq خ±] [AddCommGroup خ±] {a b : خ±} (hab : a â‰  b) :
   183â†’    Set.IsAPOfLength {a, b} 2 :=

   185â†’

   187â†’    Set.IsAPOfLength {a, b} 2 :=

   189â†’
   190â†’/-- The empty set is not an arithmetic progression of positive length. -/

   192â†’    آ¬Set.IsAPOfLength (âˆ… : Set خ±) l :=

   194â†’
   195â†’/-- We say that a set `s` is free of arithmetic progressions of length `l` if `s` contains no
   196â†’non-trivial arithmetic progressions of length `l`. Written as `Set.IsAPOfLengthFree s l`. --/
   197â†’def Set.IsAPOfLengthFree (s : Set خ±) (l : â„•âˆ‍) : Prop :=
   198â†’  âˆ€ t âٹ† s, t.IsAPOfLength l â†’ l â‰¤ 1

   200â†’/-- Any set is free of arithmetic progressions of length `1`, because such APs are all trivial. -/
   201â†’theorem Set.isAPOfLengthFree_one (s : Set خ±) : s.IsAPOfLengthFree 1 := by

   205â†’theorem Set.isAPOfLengthFree_zero (s : Set خ±) : s.IsAPOfLengthFree 0 := by

   208â†’/-- Any non-trivial arithmetic progression cannot be free of arithmetic progressions. -/
   209â†’theorem Set.IsAPOfLength.not_isAPOfLengthFree {s : Set خ±} {l : â„•âˆ‍}
   210â†’    (hs : s.IsAPOfLength l) (hl : 1 < l) : آ¬s.IsAPOfLengthFree l := by

   221â†’  simp only [maxCard, Nat.cast_zero, isAPOfLengthFree_zero, exists_const, exists_prop]

   223â†’  refine âں¨âں¨Finset.Icc 1 N, Subset.rfl, Nat.card_Icc _ _âں©, ?_âں©
   224â†’  rintro n âں¨S, hS, rflâں©

   228â†’  nth_rw 2 [â†گ maxCard_zero N]

   231â†’/-- A set `A` contains an arithmetic progression of length `k` with difference `d`. -/
   232â†’def Set.ContainsAP (A : Set خ±) (k : â„•) (d : خ±) : Prop :=

   236â†’    (coloring : M â†’ خ؛) (k : â„•) : Prop :=

   240â†’/--
   241â†’A function `f : خ± â†’ خ²` has a monotone `k`-term arithmetic progression if there exists an
   242â†’arithmetic progression `l` of length `k` in `خ±` such that its image under `f` is sorted.

   244â†’def HasMonotoneAP {خ² : Type*} [Preorder خ²] (f : خ± â†’ خ²) (k : â„•) : Prop :=
   245â†’  âˆƒ l : List خ±, l.IsAPOfLength k âˆ§ (l.map f).Pairwise (آ· < آ·)

   247â†’/--
   248â†’Define the largest possible size of a subset of a finset `s` that does not contain

    19â†’/-!
    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 156
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/156](https://www.erdosproblems.com/156)
    23â†’
    24â†’A question of Erdإ‘s, Sأ،rkأ¶zy, and Sأ³s [ESS94]. Ruzsa [Ru98b] constructed a maximal Sidon set
    25â†’of size $\ll (N \log N)^{1/3}$, but whether $O(N^{1/3})$ is achievable remains open.
    26â†’
    27â†’[ESS94] Erdإ‘s, P., Sأ،rkأ¶zy, A., and Sأ³s, V. T.
    28â†’
    29â†’[Ru98b] Ruzsa, I. Z.
    30â†’-/
    31â†’
    32â†’open Filter
    33â†’
    34â†’open scoped Topology Real
    35â†’
    36â†’namespace Erdos156
    37â†’
    38â†’/-- A finite set of natural numbers is a Sidon set (also called a $B_2$ set) if all
    39â†’pairwise sums $a + b$ (allowing $a = b$) are distinct: whenever $a + b = c + d$
    40â†’with $a, b, c, d \in A$, we have $\{a, b\} = \{c, d\}$ as multisets. -/
    41â†’def IsSidonSet (A : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    42â†’  âˆ€ a âˆˆ A, âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, âˆ€ c âˆˆ A, âˆ€ d âˆˆ A,
    43â†’    a + b = c + d â†’ (a = c âˆ§ b = d) âˆ¨ (a = d âˆ§ b = c)
    44â†’
    45â†’/-- A Sidon set $A \subseteq \{0, \ldots, N-1\}$ is maximal (in $\{0, \ldots, N-1\}$) if no
    46â†’element of $\{0, \ldots, N-1\} \setminus A$ can be added to $A$ while preserving the Sidon
    47â†’property. -/
    48â†’def IsMaximalSidonSet (N : â„•) (A : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    49â†’  A âٹ† Finset.range N âˆ§
    50â†’  IsSidonSet A âˆ§
    51â†’  âˆ€ n âˆˆ Finset.range N, n âˆ‰ A â†’ آ¬IsSidonSet (insert n A)
    52â†’
    53â†’/--
    54â†’Erdإ‘sâ€“Sأ،rkأ¶zyâ€“Sأ³s Conjecture (Problem 156) [ESS94]:
    55â†’
    56â†’Does there exist a maximal Sidon set $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ of size $O(N^{1/3})$?
    57â†’
    58â†’The greedy algorithm produces a maximal Sidon set of size $\gg N^{1/3}$ (this is known).
    59â†’Ruzsa [Ru98b] constructed a maximal Sidon set of size $\ll (N \log N)^{1/3}$, which is
    60â†’close but does not reach $O(N^{1/3})$.
    61â†’
    62â†’Formalized as: there exists a constant $C > 0$ such that for all sufficiently large $N$,
    63â†’there exists a maximal Sidon set $A \subseteq \{0, \ldots, N-1\}$ with
    64â†’$|A| \leq C \cdot N^{1/3}$.
    65â†’-/
    66â†’@[category research open, AMS 5 11]
    67â†’theorem erdos_156 : answer(sorry) â†”
    68â†’    âˆƒ C : â„‌, 0 < C âˆ§
    69â†’      âˆ€ل¶  N : â„• in atTop,
    70â†’        âˆƒ A : Finset â„•, IsMaximalSidonSet N A âˆ§
    71â†’          (A.card : â„‌) â‰¤ C * (N : â„‌) ^ ((1 : â„‌) / 3) := by
    72â†’  sorry
    73â†’
    74â†’end Erdos156

    19â†’/-!
    20â†’# Erdإ‘s Problem 154
    21â†’
    22â†’*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/154](https://www.erdosproblems.com/154)
    23â†’
    24â†’Let $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be a Sidon set with $|A| \sim N^{1/2}$.
    25â†’Must the sumset $A + A$ be well-distributed over all small moduli?
    26â†’
    27â†’[ESS94] Erdإ‘s, P., Sأ،rkأ¶zy, A., and Sأ³s, V. T., 1994.
    28â†’
    29â†’[Li98] Lindstrأ¶m, B., 1998.
    30â†’
    31â†’[Ko99] Kolountzakis, M., 1999.
    32â†’-/
    33â†’
    34â†’open Filter
    35â†’
    36â†’open scoped Topology Real
    37â†’
    38â†’namespace Erdos154
    39â†’
    40â†’/-- A finite set of natural numbers is a Sidon set (also called a $B_2$ set) if all
    41â†’pairwise sums $a + b$ (allowing $a = b$) are distinct: whenever $a + b = c + d$
    42â†’with $a, b, c, d \in A$, we have $\{a, b\} = \{c, d\}$ as multisets. Equivalently,
    43â†’all differences $a - b$ with $a \neq b$ and $a, b \in A$ are distinct. -/
    44â†’def IsSidonSet (A : Finset â„•) : Prop :=
    45â†’  âˆ€ a âˆˆ A, âˆ€ b âˆˆ A, âˆ€ c âˆˆ A, âˆ€ d âˆˆ A,
    46â†’    a + b = c + d â†’ (a = c âˆ§ b = d) âˆ¨ (a = d âˆ§ b = c)
    47â†’
    48â†’/-- The sumset $A + A = \{a + b \mid a, b \in A\}$. -/
    49â†’def sumset (A : Finset â„•) : Finset â„• := Finset.imageâ‚‚ (آ· + آ·) A A
    50â†’
    51â†’/-- The fraction of elements in a finite set of naturals that are congruent to $r$ modulo $m$. -/
    52â†’noncomputable def modFraction (m r : â„•) (S : Finset â„•) : â„‌ :=
    53â†’  ((S.filter (fun n => n % m = r)).card : â„‌) / (S.card : â„‌)
    54â†’
    55â†’/--
    56â†’Erdإ‘s Problem \#154 [ESS94]:
    57â†’
    58â†’Let $A \subset \{1, \ldots, N\}$ be a Sidon set with $|A| \sim N^{1/2}$. Must $A + A$ be
    59â†’well-distributed over all small moduli? In particular, must about half
    60â†’the elements of $A + A$ be even and half odd?
    61â†’
    62â†’Proved in the affirmative. Lindstrأ¶m [Li98] showed that $A$ itself is
    63â†’well-distributed modulo small integers (e.g. $|A \cap \{\text{evens}\}| \approx |A|/2$),
    64â†’subsequently strengthened by Kolountzakis [Ko99]. The extension to $A + A$
    65â†’follows immediately from the Sidon property: if $A$ has $e$ even and $o$ odd
    66â†’elements, then $A + A$ has exactly $e(e+1)/2 + o(o+1)/2$ even elements
    67â†’and $eo$ odd elements (all distinct by the Sidon property), and the
    68â†’distribution is approximately $1/2$ each when $e \approx o \approx |A|/2$.
    69â†’
    70â†’Formalized as: for any sequence $(A_n)_n$ of Sidon sets $A_n \subset \{0, \ldots, n\}$
    71â†’with $|A_n| / \sqrt{n} \to 1$ as $n \to \infty$, and any fixed modulus $m \geq 1$ and
    72â†’residue $0 \leq r < m$, the fraction of elements of $A_n + A_n$ in residue
    73â†’class $r \bmod m$ tends to $1/m$.
    74â†’-/
    75â†’@[category research solved, AMS 5 11]
    76â†’theorem erdos_154 : answer(True) â†”
    77â†’    âˆ€ (A : â„• â†’ Finset â„•),
    78â†’      (âˆ€ n, IsSidonSet (A n)) â†’
    79â†’      (âˆ€ n, (A n) âٹ† Finset.range (n + 1)) â†’
    80â†’      Tendsto (fun n => ((A n).card : â„‌) / Real.sqrt n) atTop (ً‌“‌ 1) â†’
    81â†’      âˆ€ (m : â„•), 1 â‰¤ m â†’
    82â†’        âˆ€ r < m,
    83â†’          Tendsto (fun n => modFraction m r (sumset (A n))) atTop (ً‌“‌ (1 / (m : â„‌))) := by
    84â†’  sorry
    85â†’
    86â†’end Erdos154
    87â†’
